tx.retrying_collision: A targeted UTxO was just consumed. Retrying with a fresh pick (attempt {{attempt}})…
tx.busy_detail: The protocol is currently busy: every targeted UTxO was consumed by another transaction. Wait a few seconds and submit again.

errors.network: Network error. Check your connection and try again.
errors.blockfrost_auth: Blockfrost rejected the request. Your VITE_BLOCKFROST_PROJECT_ID looks invalid for this network.
errors.blockfrost_rate: Blockfrost rate-limited the request. Wait a few seconds and try again.
errors.blockfrost_other: Blockfrost is having a moment. Try again in a few seconds.
errors.cbor: Transaction encoding failed. This is a bug, please report it.
errors.script_eval: On-chain script rejected the transaction. The pool or fee shard may have changed since you loaded the page. Refresh and retry.
errors.no_utxos: Your wallet has no spendable UTxOs for this transaction.
errors.no_fee_shards: Every fee shard is currently being spent. Wait a few seconds for them to settle and try again.
errors.collateral_unreachable: Collateral provider unreachable. Wait for it to recover and try again.

error.boundary_lede: An unexpected error broke this screen. The protocol is unaffected. Your boxes are still on chain. Reload to recover. If the same error keeps showing up, clear local state and try again.

home.lede: A Cardano-native privacy mixer with fixed-denomination boxes and N-way sigma-OR re-randomization.
home.pillar_one_copy: Lock the fixed denomination into a fresh mix-box.
home.pillar_two_copy: Re-randomize N boxes in a single tx.
home.pillar_three_copy: Spend a Schnorr proof to a stealth address.

config.advanced_lede: These overrides only apply on this browser, only while ?advanced=1 is in the URL. Production users never see this panel.
config.blockfrost_help: Free key at blockfrost.io. Required for chain queries + tx submission.
config.backend_url_help: Self-hosted indexer URL. Leave empty to query Blockfrost directly.
config.collateral_endpoint_help: Defaults to giveme.my. Override only if running your own provider.
config.missing_addresses: addresses.{{network}}.json missing in ui/public/. Copy from artifacts/{{network}}/.

wallet.modal_lede: Your wallet does three things by default: one signature to derive your vault, plus a signature for each deposit and each withdraw. Mix txs are wallet-anonymous in shard mode (the default), so your wallet stays off the tx entirely.
wallet.no_wallets: Install Lace, Eternl, Nami, or Flint, then reload this page.
wallet.network_mismatch: Wallet is on {{walletNet}} but this app is configured for {{appNet}}. Switch your wallet's network or pick a different account.
wallet.insufficient_balance: Wallet balance is {{have}} ₳. This transaction needs at least {{need}} ₳ for fees. The build will likely fail.
wallet.disconnect_lede: The session vault will lock and the next reconnect will re-prompt for a signature. Boxes are unaffected. They live on chain.

pool.lede: Anyone can mix the pool; there's no operator. Each Mix tx re-randomizes N pool boxes in one go, and you can pay for it two ways. Pay from the shared fee shard and your wallet stays off the tx entirely (wallet-anonymous). Or pay from your wallet and the tx is faster, but it carries your wallet identity.
pool.width_help: Linkage probability per round: 1/{{n}}. Larger N means a bigger tx + more verifier work, with empirical max set by the deployment ({{cap}}).
pool.width_help_fixed: Deployment is calibrated for N = {{n}}. Linkage probability is 1/{{n}} per round; submit again for compounding privacy.
pool.mix_disabled_collateral: Collateral provider unreachable. Mix is disabled.
pool.mix_disabled_pool: Pool too small ({{have}} of {{need}} boxes loaded).
pool.mix_disabled_wallet_needed: Wallet-fee mode requires a connected wallet. Connect a wallet, or switch to Fee shard.
pool.fee_payer_shard_hint: Default. The fee comes from the shared on-chain pool (capped at {{cap}} ADA per mix), and collateral comes from the configured collateral service. Your wallet stays off the tx entirely.
pool.fee_payer_wallet_hint: Your wallet pays the fee and supplies collateral. No on-chain cap, but your wallet identity is on the tx.
pool.tx_preview_fee_shard: Fee paid by the fee_contract shard. Collateral provided by the configured collateral service. Your wallet is not involved in this tx.
pool.tx_preview_fee_wallet: Fee paid by your wallet, and your wallet also supplies the collateral. Your wallet identity is on the tx.
pool.review_selection_value: {{n}} boxes selected at random from {{pool}} live
pool.review_fee_path_shard: Fee shard · your wallet is not on the tx
pool.review_fee_path_wallet: Your wallet · your wallet identity is on the tx
pool.review_collateral_value_shard: Provided by the configured collateral service
pool.empty_hint: No boxes have been deposited yet on this network. Make a deposit to seed the pool. Once at least 2 boxes are live, anyone can mix.
pool.confirm_lede_shard: Lovejoin will pick N random boxes, request collateral from the configured service, and submit the Mix tx. In shard mode you don't sign anything; the wallet stays off the tx.
pool.confirm_lede_wallet: Lovejoin will pick N random boxes and submit the Mix tx. Your wallet will pay the fee, supply the collateral, and sign.

fanout.lede: Chain several Mix txs into a depth-k tree starting from one of your own boxes. After each wave, every box you touched is re-mixed with two fresh pool boxes. Your branch becomes one of 3^k indistinguishable branches with linkage probability (1/3)^k.
fanout.depth_hint: Depth {{d}} mixes the user's branch into 3^{{d}} indistinguishable branches. Linkage probability after the run: {{percent}}%.
fanout.review_total_fee_value: Up to {{ada}} ADA across all mixes
fanout.disclosure_title: You are paying for everyone in this tree
fanout.disclosure_body: Re-mixing only your own box would let an observer follow your chain trivially. The fan-out re-mixes every box your prior wave touched, including boxes you do not own. That is what makes your branch indistinguishable from the rest. You pay the fees + proof-construction cost for the whole tree.
fanout.gate_no_owned_boxes: Unlock your vault and deposit at least one box to run a fan-out from a box you own.
fanout.gate_owned_in_flight: Every owned box is still in flight from a previous tx. Wait for the next block, then try again.
fanout.gate_pool_too_small: Pool too small: {{have}} fresh boxes available, fan-out at this depth needs {{need}}.
fanout.confirm_lede: Lovejoin will submit {{count}} Mix txs touching {{boxes}} mix-boxes total, with a worst-case fee up to {{ada}} ADA. Each tx is wallet-anonymous in shard mode; the collateral service signs every tx.
fanout.progress_slow_hint: This slot is taking longer than usual. The collateral host may be retrying through a transient outage — this can add a few seconds per occurrence.
fanout.progress_dropped: {{count}} mix(es) dropped because their parent tx failed
fanout.toast_partial: Fan-out finished with errors — {{submitted}} submitted, {{failed}} dropped

deposit.lede: Lock the fixed denomination into a fresh mix-box. Your wallet derives a Lovejoin owner key on first unlock, so the same wallet recovers every deposit you make on any browser. No extra phrase to write down.
deposit.rounds_help: Higher rounds = more privacy. Funds the fee shard for future mixes.
deposit.count_help: Mint this many fresh mix-boxes in a single tx. Total locked: {{total}} ADA ({{denom}} per box).
deposit.preconditions_missing: Connect a wallet and unlock your vault to deposit.
deposit.see_vault: Box stored. See it in the Vault.
deposit.tx_preview_copy: Your wallet pays the {{denom}} ADA denomination plus a small replenish-fee top-up to the fee shard. Wallet signature is required.
deposit.tx_preview_copy_bulk: Your wallet pays {{total}} ADA ({{count}} × {{denom}} ADA) plus a replenish-fee top-up to the fee shard. One signature for the whole batch.
deposit.index_help: Vault counter for this deposit's owner secret.

donate.eyebrow: Top up the shared fee shards
donate.lede: Mix txs draw on a shared pool of fee shards. Donations top up that pool without minting a new mix-box. There is no privacy gain for you here (your wallet is on the tx), but every donation extends the runway for everyone else's mixes.
donate.shards_caption: Current per-shard balance of the shared fee pool.
donate.amount_help: Any positive amount is accepted on chain. The Replenish rule only requires the shard to gain lovelace.
donate.tx_preview_copy: Your wallet pays {{amount}} ADA into a randomly-picked fee shard. Wallet signature is required.

boxes.empty: Vault has no live owned boxes yet.
boxes.empty_hint: After a deposit confirms it appears here within a block.

mix.session_only_hint: Pick boxes from this session and mix them.
mix.need_more_boxes: Mix needs at least 2 deposited boxes. Run two deposits first.
mix.fee_payer_shard_hint: Pulls from the shared fee_contract pool. Subject to the on-chain max_fee_per_mix cap.
mix.fee_payer_wallet_hint: Submitter pays the fee directly. No on-chain cap, but the wallet identity is on the tx.

withdraw.lede: Pick a live owned box from your vault and withdraw it to a destination. The mix-box is spent by Schnorr proof, so the destination has no link back to the wallet that deposited.
withdraw.select_a_box: Select a deposited box from the list.
withdraw.select_boxes: Select one or more boxes to withdraw together.
withdraw.column_index_long: Vault index: derivation counter for this owner secret
withdraw.selection_summary: {{count}} box(es) selected. {{total}} ADA total to destination.
withdraw.no_box_selected: Select at least one vault box above to continue.
withdraw.confirm_lede: This will build and submit a withdraw tx. Verify the destination address. Once submitted, the spend cannot be reversed.
withdraw.seedelf_detected: Stealth address detected. Wallet identity will be hidden after withdraw.
withdraw.seedelf_hint: This is a key address. Your wallet identity is visible at the destination. Use a Seedelf address for stealth.
withdraw.preconditions_missing: Connect a wallet and unlock your vault to withdraw.
withdraw.tx_preview_copy: Your wallet signs to fund the tx fee and collateral. The mix-box is spent by Schnorr proof, not a wallet signature. The destination is the only address visible at the receiving end.
withdraw.review_kind_stealth: Stealth (script). Wallet identity hidden at destination.
withdraw.review_kind_regular: Regular key. Wallet identity visible at destination.
withdraw.dest_wrong_network: This address is not valid for the current network.
withdraw.dest_regular_key: Regular key address. Your wallet identity is visible at the destination. Use a Seedelf address for stealth.
withdraw.dest_stealth: Stealth (script) address. Wallet identity is hidden after withdraw.
withdraw.empty_hint: Make a deposit and wait for it to confirm. The box will appear here within a block.

collateral_provider.banner_down: The collateral provider is unreachable. Mix submission is disabled until it recovers.

provider.tooltip_synced: Self-hosted indexer is caught up to chain tip. Pool data comes from your own stack.
provider.tooltip_syncing: Self-hosted indexer is reachable but lagging chain tip. Falling back to Blockfrost until caught up.
provider.tooltip_down: Self-hosted indexer is configured but unreachable. Falling back to Blockfrost.
provider.tooltip_blockfrost: No backend URL configured. All chain queries go through Blockfrost.

protocol.title: Protocol
protocol.lede: A five-minute walkthrough of how Lovejoin actually works. The math blocks are there if you want them, but the prose alone is enough to follow the shape. The full security argument lives in the Sigmajoin paper.
protocol.primitives_p: Everything in Lovejoin is composed from these three pieces. Skim them once and the rest of this page reads cleanly.
protocol.primitive_g: g: the standard generator of BLS12-381's G1 group. It's a fixed point on the curve. The notation [k] · g means "add g to itself k times", which produces another point on the curve. Critically: given [k] · g, recovering k is the discrete-log problem, believed to be infeasible at this curve size. Every secret in Lovejoin hides behind this gap.
protocol.primitive_x: x: your owner secret. Just a random number, kept private. Lovejoin derives one per deposit deterministically from your wallet's signature, so you don't manage them by hand and the same wallet recovers them on any browser.
protocol.primitive_hash: blake2b-256: a hash function. We use it for the Fiat–Shamir transform, which collapses an interactive proof (prover and verifier exchanging messages) into a single piece of data the prover can post on chain. It shows up twice below: in the Mix proof and in the Withdraw proof.
protocol.deposit_p1: A deposit picks a fresh random number <c>d</c> and locks the fixed denomination at the mix-box script. The box carries an inline datum <c>(a, b)</c>, two 48-byte compressed curve points:
protocol.deposit_p2: Anyone watching the chain sees <c>(a, b)</c> but learns nothing about <c>x</c> or <c>d</c>: both are uniform random and the discrete log is hard. The point <c>b</c> is your <e>ownership mark</e>. Only the holder of <c>x</c> can later prove they're behind it, and that proof is what unlocks the box at withdraw time.
protocol.mix_p1: A Mix tx picks N live mix-boxes from the pool and re-randomizes each one with a fresh scalar y_i. The outputs are permuted across the inputs by a random permutation π of 0..N:
protocol.mix_p2: The crucial property: if <c>b_i = [x_i] · a_i</c> on the input side, then <c>b'_i = [x_π(i)] · a'_i</c> on the output side. The same secret <c>x</c> still unlocks the box, even though the bytes <c>(a, b)</c> have changed completely. An outsider sees N inputs and N outputs but can't tell which input became which output.
protocol.mix_p3: Of course the tx has to prove the re-randomization is honest; otherwise a malicious submitter could write garbage outputs and steal everyone's funds. Each input gets an N-way sigma-OR proof: a non-interactive zero-knowledge proof that this input was re-randomized into one of the N outputs, without saying which. The witness for the true branch is the fresh scalar y_i; the other branches are simulated. With one such proof per input, every output is accounted for, and the ownership argument from above carries through the whole batch. Fiat–Shamir binds the proofs to the specific outputs in this tx, so they can't be lifted onto a different one.
protocol.privacy_p1: After a single Mix at width <c>N</c>, an outside observer's best guess at which output is yours is <c>1/N</c>. Mixes compose: <c>k</c> independent rounds drive the linkage probability down geometrically:
protocol.privacy_p2: Lovejoin's deployed calibration: N = 3 via the shared fee shard (the wallet-anonymous path), or N = 4 when the submitter pays the fee from their own wallet. The cap is a CPU-budget concern on the validator, not a security one. At N = 3, ten rounds drives linkage to about one in 60,000, and twenty rounds to about one in 3.5 billion. At N = 4, ten rounds gets you to about one in a million, and twenty rounds to about one in a trillion.
protocol.withdraw_p1: To leave the pool you spend the mix-box at its <c>mix_box</c> script's owner branch with a <e>Schnorr proof</e> that you know the <c>x</c> behind <c>b = [x] · a</c>. Schnorr is the simplest sigma protocol: the prover commits to a random point, the verifier challenges, the prover responds. Fiat–Shamir replaces the verifier's challenge with a hash, making the whole exchange a single piece of data:
protocol.withdraw_p2: The hash that produces <c>c</c> covers <c>tx.outputs</c>, which means the proof is bound to the exact destination addresses you signed for. Substituting outputs invalidates <c>c</c>, so a malicious submitter can't redirect your withdrawal anywhere you didn't ask. There is no wallet signature on the box itself; only the destination outputs are tx-authorized by the submitter's wallet (which pays the fee).
protocol.further_p: Full construction + security proofs are in the <paper>Sigmajoin paper</paper>. The on-chain verifier is in <c>contracts/validators/mix_logic.ak</c>; the off-chain builder is <c>offchain/src/tx/mix.ts</c>; cross-language test vectors are in <c>crypto/test-vectors/</c>.

vault.title: Vault
vault.lede: Sign a single message with your wallet. Lovejoin derives every owner secret deterministically from that signature. Recovery on a fresh browser is one click. No phrase to write down by default.
vault.locked_lede: Lovejoin derives your owner key from a wallet signature. The signature stays in this browser tab. It's never sent to a server, and locking the vault wipes it from memory. Only your wallet can produce this signature.
vault.scanning_pool: Scanning the pool for owned boxes…
vault.scan_summary: Found {{count}} owned box(es) in {{pool}} pool boxes.
vault.empty_hint: Make a deposit to populate the vault. The pool is rescanned each time the vault is unlocked.
vault.recover_lede: For wallets that don't expose signData, and as a cross-device recovery path. Your password and your wallet's stake address feed an Argon2id key derivation; nothing is written to disk. The same wallet + same password on any browser produces the same seed.
vault.recover_warning_title: Your password is the entire security barrier
vault.recover_warning_detail: Anyone who knows your wallet's public address can offline-brute-force this password. Use at least {{min}} characters from a mix of cases, digits, and symbols, or a 5+ word random passphrase. Lovejoin cannot recover a forgotten password.
vault.recover_password_hint: Minimum {{min}} characters. Stronger is better. Argon2id takes about 2 seconds either way.
vault.column_rounds_long: Mix rounds: how many times this box has been re-randomized since it was deposited.
vault.mix_row_confirm_lede: Submits a shard-fee mix tx that includes this box plus {{n}} - 1 other boxes picked at random from the live pool. Wallet stays off the tx; the fee is paid by a fee shard. The rounds counter for this box increases by one once the tx confirms.
vault.mix_disabled_collateral: Collateral provider is unreachable. Mix submission is disabled.
vault.mix_disabled_pool: Pool too small to mix this box. Need {{need}} live boxes; have {{have}}.

secret_card.title: Save this secret now
secret_card.warning: Without this secret no one, including you, can withdraw the box. The vault has stored an encrypted copy under your passphrase. Copy or download a backup as a second-line defense.
secret_card.label: Label

box.title: Box detail
box.linkage_explainer: Each Mix tx re-randomizes the box; after k mixes at width N the linkage probability is (1/N)^k.
box.not_found: No box found at that ref. It may have been withdrawn, mixed away, or never imported.
box.preconditions_missing: Connect a wallet and unlock the vault to withdraw.

help.title: Help
help.lede: Three short docs aimed at non-developers. The user guide walks through what Lovejoin is and how mixing works; the FAQ covers the questions that come up most often; the glossary translates the words the rest of the UI uses.

guide.intro_p1: This is the plain-language version. If you want the math and the implementation details, head to the <a1>protocol page</a1>.
guide.what_p1: Lovejoin is a privacy mixer for Cardano. You put a fixed amount of ADA into a shared pool, the pool keeps shuffling itself in public, and when you withdraw the destination has no on-chain link back to the wallet that deposited.
guide.what_p2: There is no operator. The contracts are deployed and frozen. Anyone can run the website, anyone can run the indexer, and anyone can push the "mix" button to shuffle the pool. The system pays for its own transaction fees out of a shared on-chain pot that every deposit tops up.
guide.mixing_p1: Three things happen, in any order, all the time:
guide.mixing_li1: Deposit. You lock the fixed denomination (currently 10 ADA) into a fresh "mix-box" at the protocol's script address. The box is tagged with two short cryptographic values that mark it as yours, without revealing which wallet you came from. Your wallet signs the deposit, so the deposit itself is public information.
guide.mixing_li2: Mix. Anyone, including you, can pick N live boxes from the pool and submit a single transaction that swaps their tags around using fresh randomness. The boxes still belong to the same owners afterwards, but the bytes that mark them have changed completely. An outside observer sees N inputs go in and N outputs come out, and cannot tell which input became which output. Today's deployment supports N up to 3 in the wallet-anonymous path and N up to 4 when the submitter pays the fee from their own wallet.
guide.mixing_li3: Withdraw. You spend one of your boxes to a destination address using a short cryptographic proof that you are the original owner. The proof does not require a wallet signature on the box itself, so the destination address has no on-chain relationship to the wallet that originally deposited.
guide.mixing_p2: Privacy compounds. After one mix at width N, the chance an observer guesses which output is yours is 1/N. After k mixes it is (1/N)^k. At N=3, ten rounds drives that linkage probability below one in 60,000.
guide.fee_p1: A Lovejoin mix transaction normally costs a few cents of ADA in network fees. There are two paths:
guide.fee_li1: Fee shard (default). The fee is paid out of a shared on-chain pot of about ten "fee shards" that every deposit tops up. Submitting a mix this way means your wallet is not on the transaction at all. This is the wallet-anonymous path.
guide.fee_li2: Wallet. Your wallet pays the fee directly. The transaction is faster and not capped by the on-chain limit, but your wallet identity appears as the submitter. Choose this only if you do not need wallet anonymity for that particular shuffle (for example, you are mixing other people's boxes as a public good, or your N exceeds the shard cap).
guide.fee_p2: Either way, the deposit and withdraw transactions are paid by your own wallet. Privacy is built up over time by repeated shuffles, not by hiding the deposit itself.
guide.privacy_li1: After enough mixes, the destination of your withdrawal has no on-chain link to the wallet you deposited from.
guide.privacy_li2: Mix transactions in the default path do not include your wallet as an input or signer, so the act of submitting a shuffle does not reveal which boxes are yours.
guide.privacy_li3: The contracts cannot be upgraded, frozen, or seized. There is no admin key.
guide.privacy_li4: Hiding the destination address itself. If you withdraw to a regular Cardano address you control, an off-chain observer (a block explorer with KYC labels, a chain-analysis vendor) can still recognize that address as yours. Combine Lovejoin with Seedelf if you want the destination to also be unlinkable.
guide.privacy_li5: Network-level privacy. Your IP address is visible to whichever RPC node you query and whichever website hosts the UI. Run the UI yourself, or use Tor or a VPN, if your threat model includes network observers.
guide.privacy_li6: Protection against side channels. Timing patterns ("you deposited at noon, you withdrew at 12:05") and amount patterns (you only ever deposit one box) leak information that the cryptography cannot fix. Wait, deposit more than once, mix more than the minimum.
guide.vault_p1: The vault is the in-browser bookkeeping that tracks which mix-boxes belong to you. It is a list of derivation indices, not a list of secrets you write down on paper.
guide.vault_p2: Lovejoin derives every owner secret deterministically from a single signature your wallet produces when you first unlock the vault. The same wallet on a different browser, a different machine, or after clearing local storage will produce the same signature and recover the same secrets. You do not need a backup phrase. Your wallet is the backup.
guide.vault_p3: If your wallet does not support the data-signing standard (<a1>CIP-30 signData</a1>) (some hardware-wallet integrations do not), you can fall back to a recovery password. In that mode your password plus your wallet's public stake address feed an Argon2id key derivation. The password is the entire security barrier in that mode, so make it strong (at least 5 random words, or 16+ characters of mixed case + digits + symbols). Lovejoin cannot recover a forgotten password.
guide.trouble_p1: The protocol itself cannot lose your funds. If anything goes wrong during a transaction, the worst case is that the transaction fails and your boxes stay where they were. The cryptography either verifies or it does not; there is no partial state on chain.
guide.trouble_p2: Things that can fail in practice:
guide.trouble_li1: Wallet rejects the transaction. You declined the prompt, or your wallet is on a different network than this app. Re-check the wallet's network and try again.
guide.trouble_li2: Pool is too small. A mix needs at least two live boxes (and at most as many as the deployment supports). If only your single box is in the pool, deposit a second one or wait for someone else to deposit.
guide.trouble_li3: Fee shard is being spent. Every mix transaction temporarily locks one of the ten fee shards. If all ten happen to be in flight at the same instant, your submission gets rejected. Wait a few seconds and try again.
guide.trouble_li4: Collateral provider unreachable. The wallet-anonymous path needs a third-party service to supply the collateral input (giveme.my by default). If it is offline, mix submission is disabled until it recovers. Deposits and withdrawals are unaffected.
guide.trouble_li5: You closed the browser mid-mix. The transaction either made it onto the chain or it did not. Reopen the page and unlock the vault; the pool scan will show you the truth. Lovejoin does not hold any "in-flight" state of its own.
guide.trouble_li6: You forgot your recovery password. If you opted out of the wallet-derived vault and chose the password path, the password is the only way to derive your owner secrets. Lovejoin cannot reset it. Your boxes stay on chain forever, but no one can spend them.
guide.trouble_p3: When in doubt, the <a1>FAQ</a1> probably has the question already.

faq.intro_p1: Short answers. The <a1>user guide</a1> covers each topic in more detail.
faq.safe_p2: The protocol cannot lose your funds to any party other than the holder of your owner secret. The mix-box validator only releases a box to a Schnorr proof of knowledge of the secret behind it; mix transactions only re-randomize the bytes; the contracts have no admin key, no upgrade path, and no external trust. The protocol has not undergone an independent third-party audit, and there is no bug bounty program. The code is open and the math is in the paper. Use accordingly.
faq.safe_p3: The likeliest failure mode is you losing access to your owner secret. The wallet-derived vault makes this hard (your wallet is the backup), but the recovery-password fallback makes it easy if you choose a weak password and someone offline-brute-forces it against your public stake address.
faq.vault_p1: The vault is the in-browser bookkeeping that tracks which mix-boxes belong to you. It does not hold ADA. It holds a derivation counter that, combined with one signature your wallet produces on first unlock, lets Lovejoin re-derive every owner secret you ever generated.
faq.vault_p2: You do not need a backup phrase. The wallet itself is the backup: the same wallet, on any browser, will derive the same vault and recover the same boxes. Locking the vault wipes the seed from memory; unlocking it asks the wallet to sign one message again.
faq.wallet_vs_vault_li1: The wallet is the Cardano wallet you use to talk to the chain (Lace, Eternl, Nami, Flint, Yoroi). It pays the deposit, signs the deposit transaction, and pays the withdraw transaction fee.
faq.wallet_vs_vault_li2: The vault is the Lovejoin-specific bookkeeping that tracks which mix-boxes are yours. The vault is derived from a single signature your wallet produces; it does not hold ADA.
faq.wallet_vs_vault_p2: You connect the wallet first. You unlock the vault second. The vault stays unlocked until you close the tab or click "Lock".
faq.midmix_h: What happens if I close my browser mid-mix?
faq.midmix_p2: If your mix submission made it onto the chain before you closed the browser, the mix is final. Reopen the page, unlock the vault, and the pool scan will show your boxes in their new re-randomized state. Your owner secrets still unlock them; the mix only changes the public bytes on chain.
faq.midmix_p3: If your mix submission did not make it onto the chain, it never happened. There is no "in-flight" state to recover. Reopen the page and submit again.
faq.midmix_p4: The same applies to deposits and withdraws. Cardano transactions are atomic: they either confirm or they do not. There is no partial state for Lovejoin to clean up.
faq.n_caps_p1: N is the number of boxes a single mix transaction shuffles. The upper limit is set by Cardano's per-transaction CPU budget. The sigma-OR proof that proves the shuffle is honest grows quadratically (roughly 2N² curve operations), and the budget runs out somewhere between N=4 and N=5 today.
faq.n_caps_p2: Because two paths exist for a mix transaction (paying from a fee shard or paying from the submitter's wallet), there are two slightly different budgets, which is why you see two caps:
faq.n_caps_li1: N up to 3 in the wallet-anonymous path. The fee shard redeemer adds a bit of extra work on top of the proof, and N=4 overshoots the budget.
faq.n_caps_li2: N up to 4 when the submitter pays the fee from their own wallet. The redeemer is simpler so there is more room for the proof.
faq.n_caps_p3: A larger N gives more privacy per round (linkage probability is 1/N), so N=4 is preferable when wallet anonymity is not needed for that particular shuffle. Both caps lift if and when Cardano raises the per-transaction execution-units limit. That is a network-level parameter change, out of Lovejoin's control.
faq.errors_li1: "Network error. Check your connection and try again." Your browser could not reach the RPC endpoint (Blockfrost, by default, or your self-hosted backend if you configured one). Hit reload, check your network.
faq.errors_li2: "Blockfrost rejected the request" or "Blockfrost rate-limited the request" or "Blockfrost is having a moment." The third-party RPC service we use for chain queries is misconfigured, throttling, or briefly down. The first variant means your VITE_BLOCKFROST_PROJECT_ID is invalid for this network. The other two will resolve themselves in a few seconds.
faq.errors_li3: "On-chain script rejected the transaction. The pool or fee shard may have changed since you loaded the page. Refresh and retry." Someone else's mix transaction landed first and consumed a UTxO Lovejoin had picked for yours. This is normal in a live pool; refresh and submit again.
faq.errors_li4: "Your wallet has no spendable UTxOs for this transaction." Your wallet has no ADA in spendable form on this network, or all of it is locked in pending transactions. Top up your wallet, or wait for pending transactions to confirm.
faq.errors_li5: "Every fee shard is currently being spent." All ten shared fee shards are temporarily locked by other in-flight mix transactions. Wait a few seconds and submit again.
faq.errors_li6: "You declined the wallet prompt." You clicked "cancel" or "reject" in your wallet's signature popup. Click submit again and accept it.
faq.errors_li7: "Collateral provider unreachable. Mix is disabled." The third-party service that supplies the collateral input for the wallet-anonymous path is offline. Mix submission is disabled until it recovers; deposits and withdrawals are unaffected.
faq.errors_li8: "Wallet network does not match the app's network." Switch your wallet's network to match this app, or pick a different account in your wallet that is on the right network.
faq.errors_li9: "Pool too small." The deployment supports a minimum N=2 for any mix. If only one live box is in the pool, deposit a second one or wait for someone else to deposit.
faq.errors_li10: "Transaction encoding failed. This is a bug, please report it." This is a Lovejoin bug. Open an issue at github.com/logical-mechanism/Lovejoin with the network, the operation you were doing (deposit, mix, withdraw), and any details from the browser console.

glossary.intro_p1: Plain-language definitions of the terms that show up in the Lovejoin UI.
glossary.box_p1: A single deposit. One box equals one fixed amount of ADA locked at the protocol's script address, tagged with two short cryptographic values that mark it as yours. Boxes are the only objects the Lovejoin pool contains.
glossary.deposit_p1: The first step. Your wallet locks the fixed denomination into a fresh mix-box at the protocol's script address. The deposit transaction is paid by your wallet and is public information; the privacy comes from the mixes that happen afterwards.
glossary.denomination_p1: The fixed amount of ADA every mix-box holds. Today's deployment uses 10 ADA per box. Fixed denominations are how mix-boxes become indistinguishable from each other; if every box holds a different amount, mixing is pointless.
glossary.fee_shard_p1: One of about ten shared on-chain UTxOs that pay for mix transaction fees. Every deposit tops one of them up, every mix that uses the wallet-anonymous path consumes a small amount from one. There are several of them so that multiple mixes can run in parallel without waiting on each other.
glossary.linkage_probability_p1: The chance an outside observer correctly maps a specific input box to a specific output box of a mix. After one mix at width N, the linkage probability is 1/N. After k mixes it is (1/N)^k. Lower is better.
glossary.mix_p1: The middle step. A transaction that picks N live boxes from the pool and re-randomizes them so an outsider cannot tell which input became which output. Anyone can submit a mix; the boxes still belong to their original owners afterwards. "Mixing" and "shuffling" are used interchangeably in this UI.
glossary.n_width_p1: How many boxes a single mix transaction shuffles. Today's deployment supports N=2 through N=3 in the wallet-anonymous path, and N=2 through N=4 when the submitter pays the fee from their own wallet. Larger N gives more privacy per round.
glossary.owner_secret_p1: The secret number that lets you spend a particular box. Lovejoin derives one per deposit deterministically from a single wallet signature, so you do not manage them by hand. The vault tracks the indices; the secrets themselves are computed when needed and never written to disk.
glossary.pool_p1: The set of all live mix-boxes at a given denomination. The pool is public on chain. Anyone can read it, anyone can mix it.
glossary.recovery_password_p1: An optional fallback for unlocking the vault on wallets that do not support data signing. The password plus your wallet's public stake address feed an Argon2id key derivation. The password is the entire security barrier in this mode. Use a strong one or stick to the wallet-derived path.
glossary.vault_p1: The in-browser bookkeeping that tracks which mix-boxes belong to you. It is a list of derivation indices, not a list of secrets. The vault is locked when the tab closes; unlocking it asks your wallet to produce a single deterministic signature.
glossary.wallet_anonymous_p1: A property of mix transactions. A mix is wallet-anonymous if no wallet appears as an input or signer of the transaction. Lovejoin achieves this by paying the fee from a fee shard and letting an external collateral provider supply the required collateral input.
glossary.withdraw_p1: The last step. You spend one of your boxes to a destination address using a Schnorr proof of knowledge of the owner secret. No wallet signature is needed on the box itself, so the destination has no on-chain link back to the wallet that originally deposited.
